touches.ai_summary: Cold outreach to Sebastian Hernandez at HarborIntelligence. Brief intro call establishing context around AI-powered conversation intelligence for healthcare IT sales teams. Sebastian expressed openness to learning more given recent challenges with rep ramp time.

questions_asked.question: Are you currently using any tools to help your CS team document and follow up on customer conversations?
questions_asked.answer: We're mostly manual right now—reps take their own notes. It's inconsistent and we lose a lot of context between handoffs.

next_steps: Sebastian to check calendar and propose time for deeper conversation next week

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email recapping cold call and sharing one-pager on conversation intelligence use cases for healthcare IT teams. Included case study from similar-sized company that reduced onboarding time by 40%.

questions_asked.question: Do you integrate with our existing CRM and support platforms?
questions_asked.answer: Yes—we have native integrations with all major CRMs including Salesforce and HubSpot, plus Zendesk, Intercom, and most ticketing systems.

next_steps: Sebastian confirmed interest in 15-min discovery call Thursday 2pm

touches.ai_summary: Brief email confirming discovery call scheduled for Thursday. Shared agenda covering current workflow, pain points, and technical requirements. Asked Sebastian to invite any team members who'd be involved in evaluation.
next_steps: Discovery call confirmed for Thursday 2pm PT
Sebastian to consider inviting CTO or ops lead

touches.ai_summary: Discovery call with Sebastian. Discussed current state: 27-person team with no formal call intelligence, heavy reliance on manual note-taking causing inconsistent customer records. Sebastian recently promoted to VP CS (4 months in role) and tasked with scaling team productivity. Expressed interest in automations + consulting tier given limited internal resources.

questions_asked.question: What's driving the urgency to solve this now?
questions_asked.answer: We're planning to double the CS team in Q3 and our current process won't scale. I need to show the executive team we can onboard faster and maintain quality.

questions_asked.question: Who else would need to be involved in evaluating and approving a solution like this?
questions_asked.answer: Ultimately our CFO approves any new software spend, but I have budget authority up to $5K annually. For implementation, our CTO would want to review data security.

questions_asked.question: How does your pricing work for a team our size?
questions_asked.answer: We charge per seat annually. For 7 seats on our automations plus consulting tier, list price would be around $7,400 but we have flexibility for right-fit customers.

objections: Budget is tight this quarter—we'd need to see clear ROI to justify net-new spend
next_steps: Schedule 30-min demo for next week showing automation workflows
Rep to send pricing summary and ROI calculator

touches.ai_summary: Product demo with Sebastian walking through AI call summaries, automated CRM logging, coaching insights, and custom playbook features. Sebastian particularly engaged with automated follow-up email drafting and analytics dashboards. Asked several questions about implementation timeline and compared capabilities to Fathom, which another team member had demoed previously.

questions_asked.question: We looked at Fathom a few weeks ago—how do you differ from them?
questions_asked.answer: Fathom is great for meeting transcription, but we go deeper on workflow automation and predictive analytics. Our consulting tier also includes dedicated onboarding and custom playbook development, which is key for healthcare compliance use cases.

questions_asked.question: What does implementation typically look like? How long until we're up and running?
questions_asked.answer: Most customers are fully onboarded within 2 weeks. We'd start with integration setup, then 1-on-1 training sessions, then custom playbook configuration with our consulting team.

questions_asked.question: On a scale of 1-10, how well does this align with what you're trying to solve?
questions_asked.answer: Probably an 8. The automation piece is exactly what we need. My only hesitation is whether we can get buy-in from the exec team without them seeing it firsthand.

objections: The list pricing you shared is higher than I expected—we'd need to sharpen our pencils to make this work in our Q2 budget
next_steps: Sebastian to share demo internally with CS team
Rep to prepare proposal with pricing options including discount scenario

touches.ai_summary: Post-demo follow-up email with recording link, one-page feature summary, and security documentation for CTO review. Reiterated value prop around rep productivity and included testimonial from healthcare IT customer. Confirmed Sebastian is building internal business case.

questions_asked.question: Can you share references from other healthcare companies using the consulting tier?
questions_asked.answer: Absolutely—sending intros to two VP CS contacts at similar-sized healthcare SaaS companies who've been live for 6+ months.

next_steps: Sebastian to review proposal with finance and loop in CTO on security docs
Rep offered to join internal stakeholder meeting if helpful

touches.ai_summary: Email sharing formal proposal: 7 seats, automations_plus_consulting tier, $4,200 ACV (43% discount from list). Breakdown included monthly per-seat cost, consulting hours allocation, and 12-month commitment terms. Positioned discount as 'early adopter pricing' tied to Q2 close and willingness to be a case study partner.
next_steps: Sebastian to present proposal to CFO and CTO by end of week
Call scheduled for early next week to discuss any questions

touches.ai_summary: Executive alignment meeting—Sebastian joined with CTO but CFO was unable to attend. CTO raised questions about data retention, SOC 2 compliance, and API rate limits. Discussion focused heavily on technical implementation rather than business value. Sebastian advocated for the deal but noted CFO would need to approve final contract terms.

questions_asked.question: Where is customer call data stored and how long do you retain recordings?
questions_asked.answer: Data is stored in SOC 2 compliant AWS infrastructure in US-east region. Retention is configurable—default is 2 years but you can set custom policies down to 30 days for compliance reasons.

questions_asked.question: What happens if we need to scale beyond 7 seats mid-contract?
questions_asked.answer: You can add seats anytime at prorated cost. We'd extend the same discounted per-seat rate for any additions in year one.

questions_asked.question: Is there anything that would prevent you from moving forward if the CFO approves the budget?
questions_asked.answer: No major blockers. We'd want to finalize the MSA terms around data privacy, but assuming that's standard language we should be good to proceed.

next_steps: CTO to review MSA and security addendum
Sebastian to schedule CFO review meeting before end of month

touches.ai_summary: Negotiation call with Sebastian to finalize terms. Discussed payment structure (quarterly vs annual prepay), contract length, and whether discount pricing could extend to year two renewal. Sebastian pushed for quarterly billing due to cash flow; rep agreed to quarterly at slight premium. Also requested professional services hours be front-loaded in month one for faster onboarding.

questions_asked.question: If we do quarterly billing instead of annual prepay, does the pricing change?
questions_asked.answer: We typically add a 5% premium for quarterly billing to offset cash flow timing, but given the relationship we can hold the $4,200 ACV and just structure it as $1,050 per quarter.

questions_asked.question: Can we get the same pricing locked in for a year-two renewal if we hit certain usage metrics?
questions_asked.answer: We can include a renewal rate cap in the contract—if you hit 80% user adoption and agree to a case study, we'll guarantee no more than 10% increase at renewal.

objections: I still need CFO signature and she's been hard to pin down—may slip into early Q3 if we can't get on her calendar this week
next_steps: Rep to send updated contract with quarterly billing terms and renewal language
Sebastian to coordinate CFO signature by April 18th target close date
